content:
Good afternoon, folks. This review aims to cover today's market movements. I've learned a lot today
regarding live streaming setup, particularly with toggling between charts. Going forward, these reviews will
be live and unedited to save time, as recording and editing is too time-consuming for the planned 40 weeks
of content.

section_title: DAILY DOLLAR INDEX (DXY) REVIEW AND FAIR VALUE GAP (FVG) INTERACTION
content:
This morning, the daily Dollar Index (DXY) was within a Fair Value Gap (FVG). Price approached the top of
this FVG and touched it later in the morning, leading to a retrace lower. This indicated some downside for
Euro Dollar, Cable, and E-mini S&P, though not a large movement, it still required mindfulness.
On an hourly chart for the DXY, observe the market moving up into the top of the daily chart's FVG. The
bodies of the candles precisely respected the Fair Value Gap High. This level of precision, organization,
and boundary respect in price action suggests a higher probability of similar behavior in the future when
other factors align. The weakness observed intraday was attributed to Fed Chair Powell's statements,
described as 'smoking mirrors'.

section_title: EURO DOLLAR ANALYSIS AND BALANCED PRICE RANGE (BPR)
content:
For the Euro Dollar, we anticipated a bit more downside, followed by a market rally that attacked buy-side
liquidity. A small imbalance was noted:
- Delivery from one candle's low back up to its close.
- The next candle opened and delivered to the downside.
The low of a specific candle was 1.07619, and the high of another was 1.07622, indicating a minor
deviation. Price then returned to a specific range. It stopped precisely by bumping into a candle's high at
0.07663. This area formed a Balanced Price Range (BPR).
A BPR is where price has delivered in both directions. It may revisit the entire range but is generally not
likely to go through, especially if there's an accompanying Fair Value Gap (FVG) which will be treated later.
The chairman's remarks influenced these movements.

We will now look at the S&P. The lines plotted on the chart are not indicators but represent the Dow Jones
Mini and NASDAQ Mini on an overlay basis. This was covered in detail during the morning's live session,
and this review aims to be brief.

During the 8 AM live session, I mentioned a specific area as a potential target for the afternoon. If the
market failed to go much lower, it would likely revisit this area. We saw price 'tearing up into it' in the
afternoon, concluding the live session.
Reading price action in highly consolidated environments, especially on very small timeframes, can be
challenging. When zooming out the chart to show more detail, it becomes easier to digest price action,
preventing overreaction to tiny fluctuations, especially within a small consolidation. The best approach in
such choppy conditions is to recognize the lack of clear direction, a topic explored in the live session
regarding predicting choppy mornings.

When examining a market on a one-minute chart, you are observing too small of a market structure,
particularly when it's inside a consolidation. Micro market structure, using one-minute or sub-minute
timeframes (like 15-second or 30-second), is primarily useful when the market is:
- Moving dynamically
- Animated
- Trying to achieve a rapid objective
I explained this morning that it's difficult to get a clear read in consolidated markets, illustrating various
scenarios where attempting to trigger trades in such small ranges would be unfavorable. While not 'noise',
it's not conducive for navigation until a specific time of day arrives. This critical timing was a core lesson in
today's session.

section_title: TWITTER AS A COMMUNICATION TOOL FOR MARKET LEVELS
content:
Regarding the market's run later in the day after the live stream, I utilized Twitter to communicate my
observations. My Twitter account is a one-way communication channel, not for interaction or feedback. Its
purpose is to notify followers about:
- Levels I am observing
- Draws on liquidity where I believe the market is likely to move (up or down)
These tweets are not trade signals or invitations to trade; they are simply for real-time observation. This
allows you to follow along and tape-read even when live sessions are not active. Notifications can be
managed as per individual preference.

section_title: E-MINI S&P (ES) AND SEEK AND DESTROY PROFILE
content:
Earlier, I mentioned a five-minute candle level that I tweeted about. For new followers, my official Twitter
account link is on my YouTube channel. I referenced the February 3rd, 2023, 12:55 PM candle on a five
- minute chart for ES. By providing the exact time, I enable you to locate that specific candle and track my
observations. This serves as a teaching method to help you identify these levels yourself.
Prior to price reaching this level, I tweeted to 'note that right there'. We were trading around it, and price ran
up into it today, then came all the way back down. After it hit, I advised to 'leave it alone' and 'rest', labeling
it 'wild manipulation' and 'Seek and Destroy'. The 'Seek and Destroy' market profile is characterized by
price taking out short-term highs and lows repeatedly, leading to potential significant losses for uninformed
traders.

section_title: KEY PRICE LEVELS AND IMBALANCE RECALIBRATION
content:
The market dipped down, taking out sell-side liquidity in the morning session. I discussed the 4106.50
level, which we hit within half a point or two ticks below it, with a low of 4106.00. I tweeted about this level in
the afternoon, highlighting the opportunity it presented.
The 4106.50 level's significance was explained this morning. This range involves an imbalance. While not
clearly visible on a five-minute chart, switching to a 15-minute chart reveals the relevant candle. This blue
shaded area, where price dipped for a discount, corresponds to February 1st, 2023, at 14:30 New York
local time. All timeframes and prompts I provide are calibrated to New York local time for consistency.

section_title: NEW WEEK OPENING GAP (NWOG) AND CONSEQUENT ENCROACHMENT (CE)
content:
This imbalance on the 15-minute timeframe extends higher and should be marked on your chart. Currently,
buy-side liquidity rests at the high of this range and other areas.
I also covered the New Week Opening Gap (NWOG). The market may reach up into 4130, with a further
draw at 4134.25, which is the low of the NWOG (or Sunday's opening price). Key levels include:
- Friday's close
- Consequent Encroachment (CE)
- the midpoint between Friday's close and Sunday's opening price
- Sunday's opening price
In a consolidating or non-trending market, these three specific price levels (not ambiguous 'zones') are
highly respected by price, as demonstrated by how price reacts and gravitates back to them. Even when
price moves through, observe the lows of candles respecting the Consequent Encroachment.

section_title: UNDERSTANDING SEEK AND DESTROY MARKET PROFILE
content:
Price is now through the buy-side, aiming for the upper portion of that range. The 'Seek and Destroy'
market profile describes this type of price action:
- It begins consolidated, tempting traders on small timeframes.
- Every entry attempt sees short-term highs and lows taken out repeatedly.
- A large run occurs, which then reverses completely, 'murdering' any long positions from earlier.
- Price then systematically climbs back up, potentially to areas like the one mentioned at 00:20:03.

section_title: LAST HOUR TRADING STUDY AND ANNOTATION PROTOCOL
content:
For a detailed study, focus on the last hour of trading every day. For days when I conduct a live stream, I
will not log annotations on TradingView due to the workload. Instead, use the annotations and points I
mention during the live session itself. On days without live streams, I will log observations on TradingView
for your review.

section_title: BUY SIDE IMBALANCE SELL SIDE INEFFICIENCY (BISI) AND FVG EXPANSION
content:
We observed a pullback into a Buy Side Imbalance Sell Side Inefficiency (BISI). The question is whether it
will run up and touch the high end of the 15-minute Fair Value Gap (FVG), which I refer to as a 'City'. I
previously updated this range, expanding it higher from an original five-minute chart to a 15-minute
timeframe, making it wider.

section_title: DEFINING FAIR VALUE GAP (FVG) AND ENTRY POINTS
content:
A Fair Value Gap (FVG) is defined as a single pass through a previous candle's high and the next candle's
low. The difference between these two points constitutes the FVG. When the market drops into the
Consequent Encroachment (CE) of an FVG, that is considered the highest probability entry point. Another
easy, guaranteed entry is the Institutional Order Flow Entry Drill, which is at the low of the FVG or one tick
below it.
These are two specific price levels, not ambiguous 'demand zones'. We seek very specific criteria and price
levels for our entries.

section_title: HIGH PROBABILITY ENTRIES AND TARGET METRICS
content:
From 41.75 to 41.82, that's seven handles without even needing to trade to the top of the 15-minute FVG
(or 'City'). When looking for trades, I prefer opportunities that offer at least 10 handles. In this example, the
Consequent Encroachment (the middle between the candle's high and the next candle's low) was at 41.75
and a quarter. From this point up to the high of the 15-minute FVG (shaded area) is 41.85, yielding
essentially 10 handles. This is my minimum threshold when teaching; if you seek 5 handles, look for a
range with the potential for 10 handles in your favor. This specific run delivered over 10 handles.

The concepts themselves might seem complicated initially due to unfamiliar terminology, but after a few
weeks, you'll see a pattern:
- Identifying where price is likely to go.
- Waiting for a Fair Value Gap (FVG), which involves only two specific price levels.
- Looking for an Institutional Order Flow Entry Drill or Consequent Encroachment.
This framework is straightforward. The true challenge lies in reading candlesticks live, predicting their next
move, and understanding why they perform a certain way. There's no need for fear, but respect for risk is
paramount. Many attempt this too quickly, risking funded accounts and sanity by chasing results too fast.
Relax; consistency, precision, logic, and real algorithmic theory in action, all time and price-oriented, are
demonstrated daily without cherry-picking or hindsight, removing all excuses.

I have not yet decided on the next live session, but it will not be tomorrow. I will announce it via Twitter, so
please monitor my tweets for updates on Thursday or Friday's session. When we meet again, it will likely
be at 8:15 AM New York local time. I plan to use simpler, pre-annotated charts to streamline the session.
This way, I can show charts, allow for screenshots, and then move to live data. You will have all the levels
and expectations on hand, ensuring everyone is on the same page. We will continue the morning session
until I am satisfied, not necessarily until the market run ends for the day. If you appreciate these sessions
and wish to provide encouragement, a thumbs-up on this video is all that's needed. I will communicate via
Twitter tomorrow.
